reasoning: Recent FRED data indicates that EM corporate spreads spiked sharply in mid-April 2025 (reaching 2.14) but have since established a strong short-term downtrend, falling to 1.97 by May 9. Despite this recent drop, 1.97 still represents a relatively elevated level compared to the ~1.65–1.85 baseline range observed throughout late 2024 and Q1 2025. This suggests that further mean reversion downward is more likely than a resumed increase in the near term, placing the probability of an increase slightly below 50% for the near-term dates (May 18 and June 10). For the longer-term horizons (August and November), the predictability of mean reversion fades, and the likelihood of unforeseen macroeconomic shocks or market volatility pushing spreads back up becomes more significant. In adherence to best practices, as the forecast horizon extends, unpredictability dominates and the probabilities strictly converge toward 0.5.
